records.ety: [L. cohabitare; co- + habitare to dwell, to have possession of (a place), freg. of habere to have. See Habit, n. & v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cohabited; p. pr. & vb. n. Cohabiting.]

defs: To inhabit or reside in company, or in the same place or country.
quotes: The Philistines were worsted by the captived ark . . . : they were not able to cohabit with that holy thing.

defs: To dwell or live together as husband and wife.
quotes: The law presumes that husband and wife cohabit together, even after a voluntary separation has taken place between them.
notes: ☞ By the common law as existing in the United States, marriage is presumed when a man and woman cohabit permanently together, being reputed by those who know them to be husband and wife, and admitting the relationship.
